prompt:
We can't pull in the prometheus client for the agent binary (size budget), so I need a tiny in-process metrics registry we can scrape from the debug endpoint. Package metrics at the root of module go-metrics, stdlib only.

What I want:
- type Labels map[string]string
- NewRegistry() *Registry
- reg.Counter(name string, labels Labels) *Counter and reg.Gauge(name string, labels Labels) *Gauge — get-or-create: same name + same label set (regardless of map iteration order, obviously) is the SAME series; different label values are different series. And please copy the label map — half our call sites reuse one map and mutate it between calls, that must not corrupt the registry.
- Counter: Inc(), Add(delta float64). Counters are monotonic — silently drop negative deltas.
- Gauge: Set(v float64), Add(delta float64), deltas may be negative.
- A metric name is owned by one kind: asking for a Gauge under a name already registered as a Counter (or vice versa) should panic — that's a programming error we want loud at startup.
- reg.Snapshot() []Metric where Metric has Name, Kind (constants KindCounter="counter", KindGauge="gauge"), Labels, Value. Snapshot must be sorted (name first, then the sorted k=v label pairs) so the scrape output is stable, and it must be a point-in-time copy: later Incs don't change an old snapshot, and callers scribbling on a snapshot's label maps can't touch registry state.

The whole thing gets hammered from every goroutine in the agent, so all of it — get-or-create, updates, snapshots — has to be race-free and lose no increments.

metrics_test.go in the repo is the contract; don't edit it. Done means go test -race -timeout 30s ./... is green.
